phases.name: Project Setup & Deployment
phases.description: Initialize the project, configure all infrastructure, and verify the app builds and deploys successfully before any feature work begins.

tasks.title: Initialize Next.js project
tasks.description: Create a new Next.js project with App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and ESLint. Verify the default page renders at localhost:3000.
requirements: Use `npx create-next-app@latest` with App Router enabled
TypeScript must be enabled
Tailwind CSS must be configured and working
ESLint must be configured
The default Next.js page renders successfully at http://localhost:3000
npm run build completes without errors

tasks.title: Configure Prisma with PostgreSQL
tasks.description: Install Prisma, configure it to connect to a local PostgreSQL database (via Docker) and prepare for Neon in production.
requirements: Prisma is installed as a dev dependency
@prisma/client is installed as a dependency
prisma/schema.prisma exists with PostgreSQL as the provider
Database URL is read from DATABASE_URL environment variable
A docker-compose.yml provides a local PostgreSQL instance for development
npx prisma db push works against the local database
Prisma Studio launches successfully with npx prisma studio

tasks.title: Set up environment variables pattern
tasks.description: Create .env.local for local secrets and .env.example as a template for other developers.
requirements: .env.example exists with all required variable names (no real values)
.env.local is in .gitignore
Required variables: DATABASE_URL, RESEND_API_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL, EMAIL_FROM
.env.local works with the local Docker PostgreSQL instance

tasks.title: Set up Git repository
tasks.description: Initialize Git, create a proper .gitignore, and make the initial commit.
requirements: Git repository is initialized
.gitignore includes: node_modules, .next, .env.local, .env, prisma/*.db
Initial commit contains the clean project skeleton

tasks.title: Deploy to Vercel
tasks.description: Connect the repository to Vercel and verify the app deploys and runs in production. Configure Neon as the production database.
requirements: Repository is connected to Vercel (via Git integration or CLI)
Neon PostgreSQL database is created (free tier)
DATABASE_URL is set in Vercel environment variables pointing to Neon
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL is set to the Vercel deployment URL
The app deploys successfully and the default page is accessible at the Vercel URL
Subsequent git pushes trigger automatic redeployments
corner_cases: Prisma Client must be generated during the Vercel build — add `prisma generate` to the build script if needed
Neon connection may require SSL — ensure the connection string includes ?sslmode=require

tasks.title: Set up project folder structure
tasks.description: Create the folder structure for the application following Next.js App Router conventions.
requirements: app/ — pages and layouts (App Router)
app/api/ — API route handlers
components/ — reusable React components
lib/ — shared utilities, database client, auth helpers
prisma/ — schema and migrations
A shared Prisma client singleton exists in lib/prisma.ts

tasks.title: Configure Resend for email delivery
tasks.description: Set up Resend as the email provider. Obtain an API key, configure environment variables, and verify email sending works.
requirements: Create a Resend account and obtain an API key
Set RESEND_API_KEY in .env.local for local development and in Vercel environment variables for production
Verify a sender domain in Resend (or use the default onboarding@resend.dev for testing)
Confirm that the magic link email flow works end-to-end with the configured API key
In development without RESEND_API_KEY, email content is logged to the server console as a fallback

tasks.title: Verify custom sender domain in Resend
tasks.description: Configure and verify a custom sender domain in Resend so emails are sent from your own domain instead of onboarding@resend.dev.
requirements: Add your domain in the Resend dashboard under Domains
Add the required DNS records (MX, TXT/SPF, DKIM) to your domain's DNS settings
Wait for domain verification to complete in Resend
Update the 'from' address in the magic link email route to use the verified domain
Confirm emails are delivered successfully from the custom domain

phases.name: Database Schema
phases.description: Define all database models and run the initial migration. This must be complete before any feature work.

tasks.title: Create User model
tasks.description: Define the User model in Prisma schema. Users are identified solely by email.
requirements: Fields: id (UUID, auto-generated), email (unique, required), createdAt (timestamp)
Email must have a unique constraint
No password or auth-related fields

tasks.title: Create TodoList model
tasks.description: Define the TodoList model with a unique shareable hash.
requirements: Fields: id (UUID), hash (unique, 8+ character random string), title (required), ownerId (FK to User), createdAt, updatedAt
hash must have a unique constraint and an index for fast lookups
Relation: a User has many TodoLists (as owner)

tasks.title: Create TodoItem model
tasks.description: Define the TodoItem model with all required and optional fields.
requirements: Fields: id (UUID), title (required), description (optional, text), status (enum: OPEN, COMPLETED, ARCHIVED), listId (FK to TodoList), createdByUserId (FK to User — tracks who created the item without exposing email as PII), createdAt, updatedAt
status defaults to OPEN
Relation: a TodoList has many TodoItems
Deleting a TodoList cascades to delete its TodoItems

tasks.title: Create Tag model
tasks.description: Define the Tag model. Tags are scoped per-list.
requirements: Fields: id (UUID), name (required), color (optional string for UI), listId (FK to TodoList)
A tag name must be unique within a list (compound unique on name + listId)
Many-to-many relation between TodoItem and Tag
Deleting a TodoList cascades to delete its Tags

tasks.title: Create Collaborator model
tasks.description: Define the Collaborator join model linking users to shared lists with optional nicknames.
requirements: Fields: id (UUID), listId (FK to TodoList), userId (FK to User), nickname (optional string), addedAt (timestamp)
Compound unique on listId + userId (a user can only be added once per list)
Relation: a TodoList has many Collaborators, a User has many Collaborator entries
Deleting a TodoList cascades to delete its Collaborators
The list owner is NOT a Collaborator — ownership is tracked via TodoList.ownerId
corner_cases: Adding the list owner as a collaborator should be prevented at the application level
If a collaborator's User record doesn't exist yet (new email), create the User implicitly

tasks.title: Create MagicLink and Session models
tasks.description: Define models for magic link tokens and user sessions.
requirements: MagicLink fields: id (UUID), token (unique, secure random string), email (string), expiresAt (datetime), usedAt (optional datetime — null if unused)
Session fields: id (UUID), token (unique, secure random string), userId (FK to User), expiresAt (datetime), createdAt
MagicLink tokens expire after 15 minutes
Sessions expire after 7 days
A used magic link cannot be reused (check usedAt is null)

tasks.title: Run initial migration
tasks.description: Generate and apply the Prisma migration to create all tables in the local and production databases.
requirements: npx prisma migrate dev creates the migration successfully
All tables are created in the local PostgreSQL database
Prisma Client is regenerated with all types
Migration can be applied to the Neon production database

phases.name: Magic Link Authentication
phases.description: Implement passwordless authentication via magic links sent to the user's email.

tasks.title: Landing page with email entry form
tasks.description: Create the landing page where users enter their email to receive a magic login link.
requirements: Route: / (root)
Simple form with email input and submit button
Client-side email format validation before submission
On submit: POST to /api/auth/login with the email
Show success message: 'Check your email for a login link'
Show error message if the request fails
If user is already authenticated (valid session cookie), redirect to /dashboard

tasks.title: Request magic link
tasks.description: API endpoint that generates a magic link token, creates the user if needed, and sends the login email.
requirements: Route: POST /api/auth/login
Accepts JSON body: { email: string }
Validate email format on the server
If no User exists for this email, create one (implicit registration)
Generate a cryptographically secure random token (at least 32 bytes, URL-safe)
Store a MagicLink record with the token, email, and expiresAt (15 minutes from now)
Send an email via Resend containing a link: {APP_URL}/api/auth/verify?token={token}
In development (no RESEND_API_KEY), log the magic link URL to the server console instead
Return 200 with { message: 'Magic link sent' } regardless of whether the email exists (prevent email enumeration)
The sender address is read from the EMAIL_FROM environment variable (defaults to onboarding@resend.dev for development)
The Resend API response is checked for errors and failures are logged to the console
corner_cases: Multiple magic link requests for the same email should each create a new token (old ones remain valid until expiry)
Email should be lowercased and trimmed before processing
Return the same success response even for invalid emails to prevent enumeration

tasks.title: Verify magic link and create session
tasks.description: API endpoint that verifies the magic link token, creates a session, and redirects to the dashboard.
requirements: Route: GET /api/auth/verify?token={token}
Look up the MagicLink record by token
Verify the token has not expired (expiresAt > now)
Verify the token has not been used (usedAt is null)
Mark the token as used (set usedAt to now)
Find the User by the magic link's email
Create a Session record with a secure token, userId, and expiresAt (7 days from now)
Set an HTTP-only, secure, SameSite=Lax cookie named 'session' with the session token
Redirect to /dashboard
If token is invalid, expired, or already used: redirect to / with an error query param
corner_cases: Expired token should show a clear message asking the user to request a new link
Already-used token should show 'This link has already been used'
Race condition: two requests with the same token — only the first should succeed (use atomic update)

tasks.title: Session middleware
tasks.description: Create middleware or utility to validate the session cookie and provide the authenticated user to page/API handlers.
requirements: Read the 'session' cookie from the request
Look up the Session record by token
Verify the session has not expired
Return the associated User if valid, null if not
Provide a helper function: getAuthenticatedUser(request) → User | null
Protected pages (dashboard) redirect to / if not authenticated
Protected API routes return 401 if not authenticated

tasks.title: Logout
tasks.description: Allow users to end their session.
requirements: Route: POST /api/auth/logout
Delete the Session record from the database
Clear the session cookie
Redirect to /
A logout button is visible on the dashboard and list views

tasks.title: Delete account and all user data
tasks.description: Allow authenticated users to permanently delete their account and all associated data from the database.
requirements: Route: DELETE /api/auth/account
Requires authentication — return 401 if not logged in
Delete all data associated with the user: sessions, magic links, owned todo lists (with cascading items, tags, collaborators), collaborator entries on other users' lists, and the User record itself
Clear the session cookie after deletion
A 'Delete account' button is visible on the dashboard (e.g., in settings or profile area)
Show a confirmation dialog requiring the user to type their email to confirm
After deletion, redirect to /
corner_cases: Collaborators on the user's owned lists lose access when those lists are cascade-deleted
The user's collaborator entries on other users' lists should be removed but those lists remain intact
All active sessions for the user should be deleted, not just the current one
Ensure the deletion is atomic — use a database transaction so partial deletes don't occur

tasks.title: Configure verified sender domain for email delivery
tasks.description: Set up a verified sender domain in Resend and configure EMAIL_FROM so magic link emails deliver to all users, not just the account owner.
requirements: A sending domain is verified in the Resend dashboard (DNS records: MX, TXT/SPF, DKIM)
EMAIL_FROM is set in production environment to use the verified domain (e.g. 'KinkList <noreply@yourdomain.com>')
Magic link emails deliver successfully to external email addresses (not just the Resend account owner)
Notification emails (item additions) also use the configured EMAIL_FROM
The onboarding@resend.dev fallback remains for local development without Resend configuration

phases.name: Security & Privacy
phases.description: Implement security measures to protect highly sensitive PII and personal data. Given the nature of the application (sexual fantasies and kinks), data leaks could cause serious personal harm. Security must be in place before building data-serving endpoints.

tasks.title: Add security headers
tasks.description: Configure security headers via Next.js middleware or next.config to harden the application against common web attacks and prevent hash URL leakage.
requirements: Set Referrer-Policy: no-referrer — critical to prevent hash URLs leaking via referer headers to external resources
Set X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Set Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains (HTTPS only)
Set Content-Security-Policy with restrictive defaults (default-src 'self', script-src 'self', style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline')
Set X-Frame-Options: DENY to prevent clickjacking
Verify headers are present on all responses (pages and API routes)

tasks.title: Require authentication for hash URL access
tasks.description: Change the sharing model so that hash URLs require authentication. The hash URL serves as an invitation mechanism, not an open-access pass. This prevents leaked URLs from exposing sensitive content to anyone.
requirements: Navigating to /list/[hash] without a valid session redirects to / with a prompt to log in
After login, if the user is the owner or a collaborator, they see the list
After login, if the user is NOT the owner or collaborator, show a 403 'Access denied' page
The hash URL is used when adding collaborators (owner shares the link, recipient logs in to access)
Store the intended destination URL so the user is redirected back after login
Hash URLs must be at least 16 characters of crypto.randomBytes for resistance to guessing
corner_cases: A user who receives a hash URL but is not yet a collaborator should see a 'Request access' or 'Access denied' message — they cannot self-join
The redirect-after-login flow must preserve the original hash URL

tasks.title: Server-side authorization on all data endpoints
tasks.description: Every API route that reads or writes list/item/tag/collaborator data must verify the requesting user is authorized for that specific resource. Being authenticated is not sufficient — the user must be the owner or a collaborator of the specific list.
requirements: Create a reusable authorization helper: authorizeListAccess(userId, listHash) → { list, role: 'owner' | 'collaborator' } | null
Every list/item/tag/collaborator API route calls this helper and returns 403 if unauthorized
Owner-only operations (delete list, manage collaborators) additionally check role === 'owner'
All database queries are scoped to authorized lists — never trust client-provided IDs alone
Write integration tests verifying: owner can access, collaborator can access, non-collaborator gets 403, unauthenticated gets 401
corner_cases: A user who was removed as collaborator should immediately lose access
Concurrent requests during collaborator removal must not leak data

tasks.title: Enforce PII-safe display of item creators
tasks.description: The schema migration is complete (createdByUserId FK is in place via SCHEMA-003). This task covers the application-layer work: resolving createdByUserId to safe display names in API responses.
requirements: Update all item creation logic to set createdByUserId from the authenticated user
In API responses, resolve createdByUserId to the collaborator's nickname (if set) or a generic label — never expose raw email to non-owners
Only the list owner can see collaborator emails; other collaborators see nicknames only
corner_cases: If no nickname is set for a collaborator, show a generic label like 'Collaborator' rather than their email

tasks.title: Rate limiting on authentication endpoints
tasks.description: Add rate limiting to prevent email bombing, brute-force token guessing, and abuse of auth endpoints.
requirements: Rate limit POST /api/auth/login: max 3 requests per email per hour, max 10 requests per IP per hour
Rate limit GET /api/auth/verify: max 10 attempts per IP per 15 minutes (prevent token brute-force)
Return 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header when limit is exceeded
Use in-memory rate limiting for v1 (consider Redis for production scale later)
Rate limiting must not leak whether an email exists (same 429 response regardless)
corner_cases: Serverless cold starts may reset in-memory counters — acceptable for v1 but document the limitation
Consider using Vercel's built-in rate limiting or Edge Middleware for production

tasks.title: Input validation and sanitization
tasks.description: Validate and sanitize all user inputs server-side to prevent XSS, injection, and data integrity issues.
requirements: Validate all API inputs server-side (not just client-side): list titles, item titles, descriptions, nicknames, emails, tag names
Enforce maximum field lengths (e.g., title: 500 chars, description: 5000 chars, nickname: 100 chars, tag name: 50 chars)
Sanitize HTML/script content in text fields to prevent stored XSS
Reject requests with invalid or missing required fields with 400 status and descriptive error
Create a shared validation utility used by all API routes for consistency

tasks.title: Explicit field selection in API responses
tasks.description: Never return full database objects to the client. Always use Prisma select to return only the fields needed, preventing accidental PII leakage.
requirements: All Prisma queries in API routes use explicit select (not include with full objects)
User emails are never included in list/item responses unless the requesting user is the list owner
Internal IDs (database UUIDs) are not exposed unless necessary — prefer hash identifiers
Collaborator lists return nickname and role only (not email) to non-owner users
Create response-shaping helpers per entity type (e.g., toListResponse, toItemResponse) that enforce field filtering based on the requesting user's role

tasks.title: IDOR prevention in all data queries
tasks.description: Prevent Insecure Direct Object Reference attacks by always scoping database queries to the authorized user's accessible resources.
requirements: Never use a client-provided item/tag/collaborator ID in a query without also scoping to the parent list
All item operations verify the item belongs to a list the user has access to
All tag operations verify the tag belongs to a list the user has access to
All collaborator management operations verify the user is the list owner
Example pattern: prisma.todoItem.update({ where: { id: itemId, listId: authorizedListId } }) — never just { where: { id: itemId } }

tasks.title: Session security improvements
tasks.description: Harden session management given the sensitivity of the data.
requirements: Add 'Log out all devices' functionality — delete all sessions for the current user
Reduce session TTL from 7 days to 3 days given data sensitivity
Rotate session token on sensitive actions (e.g., after adding/removing collaborators)
Add session activity tracking (lastUsedAt timestamp) to enable idle timeout detection
Show active sessions on the dashboard (creation date, last used) so users can audit access

tasks.title: Audit logging for sensitive actions
tasks.description: Log security-relevant actions to enable post-incident investigation and help users detect unauthorized access.
requirements: Log the following events with timestamp, userId, and relevant metadata: list access (view), collaborator added/removed, list created/deleted, session created/destroyed, account deleted
Store audit logs in a dedicated AuditLog database table
Do NOT log item content in audit logs (the content itself is sensitive) — only log that an action occurred
Provide an API endpoint for list owners to view access history for their lists
Audit logs are retained for 90 days, then automatically purged
corner_cases: Audit logs must survive list/user deletion for the retention period (do not cascade delete)
Audit log queries must be performant — index on listId and timestamp

tasks.title: Prevent collaboration spam via join-via-link
tasks.description: Replace the add-collaborator-by-email mechanism with a join-via-link flow to prevent bots from using the collaboration system as a spam vector. Instead of the owner entering an email to add a collaborator, users must visit the shared hash URL and explicitly join. The hash URL (128-bit entropy) serves as the invitation token.
requirements: Remove the add-by-email form and API endpoint
Replace POST /api/lists/[hash]/collaborators to allow authenticated users to join themselves
Replace the 403 page with a 'Join this list' page for authenticated non-members
The owner can still remove collaborators and manage nicknames
No implicit user creation — users must register via magic link before joining
Audit log uses 'collaborator.join' action to distinguish from legacy 'collaborator.add'

phases.name: Dashboard
phases.description: The user's home screen showing all their todo lists with options to create and delete lists.

tasks.title: View dashboard with all todo lists
tasks.description: Display all todo lists the authenticated user owns or has been added to as a collaborator.
requirements: Route: /dashboard (protected — requires authentication)
Fetch all TodoLists where the user is the owner OR a collaborator
Display each list with: title, item count, creation date
Visually distinguish owned lists from shared lists (e.g., a 'shared' badge)
Show the user's email and a logout button in the header
Empty state: friendly message when the user has no lists yet

tasks.title: Create a new todo list
tasks.description: Allow users to create a new todo list from the dashboard.
requirements: A 'Create list' button or input on the dashboard
User enters a list title
On submit: create a TodoList with a generated unique hash, the title, and the authenticated user as owner
The hash must be URL-safe (alphanumeric, 8+ characters) and unique
After creation, the new list appears in the dashboard (no full page reload needed)
Optionally redirect to the new list's view
corner_cases: Empty or whitespace-only title should be rejected
Hash collision (extremely unlikely but handle): regenerate if the hash already exists

tasks.title: Delete a todo list
tasks.description: Allow users to delete a todo list they own from the dashboard.
requirements: Each owned list has a delete button/action
Show a confirmation dialog before deleting
On confirm: delete the TodoList and all associated data (items, tags, collaborators) via cascade
Only the owner can delete a list — collaborators cannot
After deletion, the list is removed from the dashboard immediately
Collaborators who had access will no longer see the list on their dashboard
corner_cases: Attempting to delete a list you don't own returns 403
Deleting a list that no longer exists returns 404 gracefully

phases.name: Todo List View
phases.description: The main interface for viewing and interacting with a single todo list.

tasks.title: View todo list page
tasks.description: Display a single todo list with all its items, accessed via the list's unique hash.
requirements: Route: /list/[hash]
Fetch the TodoList by hash, including all non-archived TodoItems
Display the list title at the top
Display each item showing: title, status (checkbox), and any set optional fields
If accessed by an authenticated user who owns or collaborates on the list, show full controls
If accessed without authentication, redirect to login with return URL preserved (SEC-002)
If accessed by an authenticated user who is NOT the owner or collaborator, show 403 page
Show a 404 page if the hash doesn't match any list
All data fetching must go through the authorization helper (SEC-003)

tasks.title: Add a new todo item
tasks.description: Allow adding new todo items to a list with at minimum a title.
requirements: An input field (or form) at the top or bottom of the list for quick item entry
Title is required — cannot submit empty
On submit: create a TodoItem with status OPEN, createdByUserId set to the authenticated user's ID (all users must be authenticated per SEC-002)
New item appears in the list immediately without full page reload
Focus returns to the input after adding for rapid entry
corner_cases: Whitespace-only titles should be rejected
Very long titles should be handled gracefully (truncate display, store full value)

tasks.title: Edit todo item title
tasks.description: Allow inline editing of a todo item's title.
requirements: Clicking on a todo item's title makes it editable (inline input)
Pressing Enter or clicking away saves the change
Pressing Escape cancels the edit and reverts to original title
Empty title is not allowed — revert to original if cleared
Update is persisted to the database

tasks.title: Edit todo item description
tasks.description: Allow editing a longer description on a todo item.
requirements: Description is shown when the item is expanded (not visible by default — see UX note)
A text area allows entering/editing the description
Description is optional — can be left empty
Changes are persisted on blur or explicit save

tasks.title: Toggle todo item status
tasks.description: Allow marking a todo item as completed or reopening it.
requirements: A checkbox or toggle next to each item
Clicking it toggles between OPEN and COMPLETED status
Completed items should have a visual indicator (strikethrough, dimmed, or similar)
Status change is persisted immediately
Completed items remain in the list (not auto-archived)

tasks.title: Delete a todo item
tasks.description: Allow removing a todo item from a list.
requirements: A delete button/icon on each item (visible on hover or in expanded view)
Show a confirmation for deletion (or support undo)
Item is permanently removed from the database
Item disappears from the list immediately

tasks.title: Archive completed items
tasks.description: Allow manually archiving completed items to declutter the list.
requirements: A button to 'Archive completed items' — only visible when there are completed items
On click: set status to ARCHIVED for all COMPLETED items in the list
Archived items are hidden from the main list view
A separate 'View archive' section or toggle shows archived items
Individual items can be unarchived (set back to OPEN)
corner_cases: Archiving when no items are completed should be a no-op (button hidden or disabled)
Unarchiving an item returns it to the main list with OPEN status

phases.name: Todo Item Optional Fields
phases.description: Optional metadata fields on todo items. These should be unobtrusive in the UI.

tasks.title: Expandable item detail view
tasks.description: Create an expand/collapse UI for each todo item to show optional fields.
requirements: Each item can be expanded to reveal: description, tags, props
Default state is collapsed — only title and checkbox visible
Clicking the item (not the checkbox) expands it
Only one item expanded at a time, or allow multiple (choose based on UX feel)
Smooth expand/collapse animation

tasks.title: Manage tags within a list
tasks.description: Allow creating, viewing, and deleting tags that are scoped to a specific list.
requirements: Tags are per-list (not global across user's lists)
A way to create new tags with a name and optional color
A way to delete existing tags from the list
Deleting a tag removes it from all items in that list
Tag names must be unique within a list
corner_cases: Empty or whitespace-only tag names should be rejected
Duplicate tag name within the same list should show an error

tasks.title: Add and remove tags on a todo item
tasks.description: Allow tagging and untagging todo items with existing list tags.
requirements: In the expanded item view, show a tag selector with available list tags
User can add one or more tags to an item
User can remove tags from an item
Tags on an item are shown as small chips/badges in both collapsed and expanded views
Changes are persisted immediately

tasks.title: Add props field to TodoItem schema
tasks.description: Add a JSON array field to TodoItem for storing required accessories, toys, gear, and outfits (called 'props').
requirements: Add props Json @default("[]") field to TodoItem in Prisma schema
Run Prisma migration
Add props to itemSelect in lib/responses.ts
Add props to toItemResponse — returned as string array
POST /api/lists/[hash]/items accepts an optional props array of strings
PATCH /api/lists/[hash]/items/[itemId] accepts an optional props array of strings
Validate that props is an array of strings, max 20 items, each max 100 characters
Sanitize prop values (strip HTML, trim whitespace)

tasks.title: Create curated prop suggestions data file
tasks.description: Create a static TypeScript file with a categorized list of common prop names for auto-suggest. This is global data maintained by us, not user-generated.
requirements: Create lib/prop-suggestions.ts exporting categorized prop suggestions
Categories include: toys, restraints, sensory, clothing/outfits, furniture, consumables, safety/aftercare
Each category has a name and an array of prop names
Export a flat array of all prop names for simple search
Export a function to search/filter suggestions by query string (case-insensitive, prefix/substring match)
Data is static — bundled with the app, no API or DB needed

tasks.title: Props UI — add/remove props on items with auto-suggest
tasks.description: Build the UI for adding and removing props on todo items, with auto-suggest powered by the curated global suggestions list.
requirements: In the expanded item view, show an input field for adding props
As the user types, show a dropdown of matching suggestions from the curated list (case-insensitive)
User can select a suggestion or press enter to add a free-text prop
Props not in the curated list can still be added — auto-suggest simply does not help for those
Added props are shown as removable chips/badges
Props are displayed in both collapsed and expanded views
Duplicate props on the same item should be prevented
Changes are persisted immediately via PATCH to the item

phases.name: Sharing & Collaboration
phases.description: Features for sharing lists via URL and managing collaborators.

tasks.title: Copy shareable URL
tasks.description: Allow users to copy the list's shareable hash URL to their clipboard.
requirements: A 'Share' or 'Copy link' button on the list view
Clicking it copies the full URL (e.g., https://app.example.com/list/a3f9b2c1) to clipboard
Show brief feedback: 'Link copied!' tooltip or toast
The URL is the same hash URL the list is already accessible at

tasks.title: Access shared list via hash URL (authenticated)
tasks.description: Hash URLs serve as an invitation/bookmark mechanism. Users must be authenticated AND be the owner or a collaborator to view the list. This protects sensitive content from being exposed via leaked URLs.
requirements: Navigating to /list/[hash] without a session cookie redirects to / with a return URL stored (so user is redirected back after login)
After login, if the user is the owner or a collaborator, they see the full list
After login, if the user is NOT the owner or collaborator, show a 403 'You do not have access to this list' page
All list operations (view, add, edit, complete, delete items) require authentication + authorization
There is no anonymous/unauthenticated access to list content
corner_cases: A user who receives a hash URL but is not a collaborator cannot self-join — they must be added by the owner
The redirect-after-login flow must preserve the original hash URL across the magic link email flow
If a user bookmarks a hash URL and their collaborator access is later revoked, they should see 403 on next visit

tasks.title: Join list via shared link
tasks.description: Allow authenticated users to join a list as a collaborator by visiting the shared hash URL. Replaces the old add-by-email flow to prevent spam abuse (SEC-008).
requirements: A 'Manage collaborators' section in the list view (visible to owner and existing collaborators)
When an authenticated user visits /list/[hash] and is not the owner or a collaborator, show a 'Join this list' page with the list title and a join button
On click: POST to /api/lists/[hash]/collaborators creates a Collaborator record for the authenticated user
After joining, the page refreshes to show the full list view
The list then appears on the collaborator's dashboard
No email-based invitation — the hash URL (128-bit entropy) serves as the invitation token
corner_cases: The list owner never sees the join page (authorizeListAccess succeeds with role 'owner')
Double-clicking join returns 400 'Already a collaborator'
A removed collaborator can re-join by visiting the link again

tasks.title: Remove collaborator
tasks.description: Allow the list owner to remove a collaborator from the list.
requirements: Each collaborator in the list has a remove button (visible to the list owner)
On click: remove the Collaborator record
The list no longer appears on the removed user's dashboard
The removed user can still access the list via the hash URL (as an unauthenticated user would)

tasks.title: Set and edit collaborator nickname
tasks.description: Allow the list owner to set a display nickname for each collaborator.
requirements: Each collaborator entry shows their email and an editable nickname field
Nickname is optional — defaults to empty (show email only)
Nickname is per-list (same user can have different nicknames on different lists)
Nickname is visible wherever the collaborator is referenced (e.g., item's createdBy)
Changes are persisted immediately

tasks.title: Notify collaborators when items are added
tasks.description: Send email notifications to collaborators when new items are added to a shared list.
requirements: When a new item is added to a list that has collaborators, send an email to all collaborators except the person who added the item
Email includes: the item title (or summary) and a direct link to the list
Notifications should be batched/debounced — if multiple items are added within a short window (e.g., 2 minutes), send one summary email instead of multiple
Use Resend for email delivery (same as magic link emails)
In development (no RESEND_API_KEY), log the notification to the server console
The list owner is also notified if a collaborator adds an item (owner is treated like any other participant)
corner_cases: All item additions are by authenticated users (per SEC-002) — 'added by' uses the collaborator's nickname or the owner label
If a list has no collaborators, no notifications are sent
Respect Resend free tier limits (100 emails/day) — consider a daily notification cap per list

tasks.title: Display collaborator list
tasks.description: Show all collaborators on a list with their emails and nicknames.
requirements: A visible section in the list view showing all collaborators
For the list owner: each entry shows nickname (if set) and email
For collaborators: each entry shows nickname only — emails are hidden from non-owners (SEC-007)
The list owner is shown separately (e.g., 'Owner: [nickname]')
Collaborator management controls (add, remove, nickname) are visible only to the list owner

phases.name: Filtering & Sorting
phases.description: Allow users to filter and sort todo items within a list.

tasks.title: Filter by status
tasks.description: Filter todo items by their status.
requirements: Filter options: All, Open, Completed, Archived
Default: show Open items (hide archived)
Selecting a filter immediately updates the displayed items
Show item count per status

tasks.title: Filter by tag
tasks.description: Filter todo items by one or more tags.
requirements: Show all available tags for the list as filter chips
Clicking a tag filters to items that have that tag
Multiple tags can be selected (show items that have ANY of the selected tags)
Can be combined with other filters

tasks.title: Sort items
tasks.description: Allow sorting todo items by various fields.
requirements: Sort options: Creation date (newest first), Status
Default sort: creation date (newest first)
Sort direction can be toggled (ascending/descending)
Sorting works in combination with active filters

tasks.title: Persist filter and sort state in URL
tasks.description: Store the current filter and sort configuration in URL query parameters so it can be bookmarked or shared.
requirements: Filter and sort selections are reflected in URL query params (e.g., ?status=open&sort=createdAt)
Loading a URL with filter params applies those filters automatically
Changing filters updates the URL without a full page reload
Default filters produce a clean URL (no redundant params)

phases.name: UX & Responsiveness
phases.description: Polish the user experience and ensure the app works well on all screen sizes.

tasks.title: Responsive layout
tasks.description: Ensure all pages work well on both mobile and desktop viewports.
requirements: Landing page, dashboard, and list view are fully usable on mobile (320px+)
No horizontal scrolling on any viewport
Touch-friendly tap targets (min 44px)
Appropriate font sizes and spacing for mobile
Desktop layout makes good use of available space

tasks.title: Loading states
tasks.description: Show appropriate loading indicators during async operations.
requirements: Loading spinner or skeleton while fetching dashboard lists
Loading spinner or skeleton while fetching list items
Disabled state on buttons while submitting (prevent double-clicks)
Optimistic UI updates where appropriate (e.g., checking off a todo)

tasks.title: Error handling and user feedback
tasks.description: Show clear error messages and confirmations for user actions.
requirements: Toast or notification system for success messages (e.g., 'List created', 'Item deleted')
Toast or inline error for failed operations (e.g., 'Failed to save, please try again')
Form validation errors shown inline next to the relevant field
Network error handling: show a message if the server is unreachable

phases.name: Encryption at Rest
phases.description: Implement per-list envelope encryption to protect all sensitive data at rest. Each list gets its own AES-256-GCM data encryption key (DEK), wrapped by a master key (KEK) stored in an environment variable. Protects against database breaches, stolen backups, and unauthorized DB access.

tasks.title: Create encryption module (lib/crypto.ts)
tasks.description: Implement the core encryption/decryption module using Node.js crypto. No new dependencies required.
requirements: generateDEK() returns 32 cryptographically random bytes
encryptDEK(dek, kek) / decryptDEK(encryptedDek, kek) wraps/unwraps DEK with master key using AES-256-GCM
encryptField(plaintext, dek) / decryptField(ciphertext, dek) encrypts/decrypts individual text fields using AES-256-GCM
encryptFields(obj, fields, dek) / decryptFields(obj, fields, dek) batch encrypt/decrypt multiple fields on an object
computeNameHash(plaintext, dek) returns HMAC-SHA256 for deterministic uniqueness checks
isEncrypted(value) detects encrypted values by checking for 'enc:v1:' prefix
Storage format: enc:v1:<base64(iv + authTag + ciphertext)> with 12-byte IV and 16-byte auth tag
KEK is read from process.env.ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY (32 bytes, base64-encoded)
getKEK() throws a clear error if ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY is not configured
Tampered ciphertext is detected and rejected by GCM authentication
Wrong key produces an error, not garbled plaintext

tasks.title: Schema changes for encryption
tasks.description: Add database columns to support per-list encryption keys and deterministic tag name hashing.
requirements: Add encryptedDEK String? column to TodoList model (nullable for phased migration)
Add nameHash String? column to Tag model
Update @@unique constraint on Tag from [name, listId] to [nameHash, listId]
Prisma migration runs cleanly against the database
Add ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY to .env.example with generation instructions
Generation command documented: node -e "console.log(require('crypto').randomBytes(32).toString('base64'))"

tasks.title: Extend withListAccess to provide DEK
tasks.description: Modify the central list access authorization helper to also fetch and decrypt the list's DEK, making it available to all route handlers.
requirements: withListAccess fetches encryptedDEK alongside existing list access check
Decrypted DEK (Buffer) is included in the ListAccess return type
When encryptedDEK is null (unencrypted legacy list), DEK is returned as null
No additional database query required — DEK is fetched in the same query
All existing route handlers continue to work without modification

tasks.title: Encrypt list titles
tasks.description: Encrypt list titles on write and decrypt on read in the lists API routes.
requirements: POST /api/lists generates a DEK, encrypts the title, and stores both encryptedDEK and encrypted title
GET /api/lists decrypts each list's title using its DEK before returning
Mixed encrypted/unencrypted data is handled gracefully (check enc:v1: prefix)
Lists without a DEK (legacy) return plaintext title as-is
New lists always have an encryptedDEK and encrypted title
Database contains ciphertext (enc:v1:...), not plaintext, for new lists
corner_cases: GET /api/lists fetches lists from both owned and collaborated — both need DEK decryption
List title in the page.tsx server component must also be decrypted

tasks.title: Encrypt item titles and descriptions
tasks.description: Encrypt item content on write and decrypt on read across all item API routes.
requirements: POST /api/lists/[hash]/items encrypts title and description before database write
GET /api/lists/[hash]/items decrypts title and description for all items
PATCH /api/lists/[hash]/items/[itemId] encrypts any updated title or description fields
Mixed encrypted/unencrypted data is handled (check enc:v1: prefix for each field)
Null/empty descriptions remain null/empty (not encrypted)
Notification emails still contain readable (decrypted) item titles
corner_cases: queueItemNotification receives the plaintext title before encryption — verify this flow
Description field is optional (@db.Text) — only encrypt if non-null

tasks.title: Encrypt collaborator nicknames
tasks.description: Encrypt collaborator nicknames on write and decrypt on read.
requirements: PATCH /api/lists/[hash]/collaborators/[id] encrypts nickname before database write
GET /api/lists/[hash]/collaborators decrypts nicknames for all collaborators
Null nicknames remain null (not encrypted)
Role-based response shaping (owner vs collaborator) still works after decryption

tasks.title: Encrypt tag names with HMAC uniqueness
tasks.description: Encrypt tag names and use HMAC-SHA256 for the uniqueness constraint, since encrypted values with random IVs are never equal.
requirements: Tag name is encrypted on create and update
Tag name is decrypted on read
nameHash is computed as HMAC-SHA256(plaintext_name, dek) on create and update
nameHash is deterministic per-list (same name + same DEK = same hash)
@@unique([nameHash, listId]) prevents duplicate tag names within a list
Tags are sorted client-side after decryption (server-side sort on encrypted values is meaningless)
corner_cases: Tag color is not encrypted (non-sensitive)
If the list's DEK is rotated, all nameHash values must be recomputed

tasks.title: Data backfill migration script
tasks.description: Create a standalone script to encrypt all existing plaintext data in the database.
requirements: Script at scripts/migrate-encrypt.ts
For each list without encryptedDEK: generate DEK, encrypt list title, encrypt all item titles/descriptions, encrypt all tag names and compute nameHash, encrypt all collaborator nicknames
Script is idempotent: skips already-encrypted fields (checks enc:v1: prefix)
Script reports progress (number of lists processed, items encrypted)
Script handles errors gracefully (logs failures, continues with next list)
After running, all data in the database is encrypted
corner_cases: Large databases should be processed in batches to avoid memory issues
Script must be run with ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY set in environment

tasks.title: Remove plaintext fallback and finalize
tasks.description: After all data is encrypted, remove the backward-compatible plaintext handling and make encryption mandatory.
requirements: Make encryptedDEK non-nullable in Prisma schema
Remove isEncrypted() / enc:v1: prefix checks from all route handlers
Remove null DEK handling from withListAccess (DEK is always present)
Prisma migration runs cleanly
All API responses still return correct plaintext data
No unencrypted data remains in the database
